BSAVA Manual of
Guinea  
Pigs
Edited by
John Chitty, Deborah Monks,  
Anna Meredith and Bob Doneley

John Chitty BVetMed CertZooMed CBiol MRSB MRCVS
John Chitty worked in exotics practice in the UK for over 30 years and developed 
a special interest in guinea pigs. He is an RCVS Advanced Practitioner in Zoological 
Medicine. He now provides consultancy services to vets, zoos and local authorities. 
John has published widely on a range of exotic species and small mammals and has 
edited several textbooks. He is a Past President of BSAVA and is Founder Chair of the 
charity Guinea Pig Welfare UK.   
Deborah Monks BVSc(Hons) CertZooMed DipECZM(Avian Medicine) 
FANZCVS(Avian Medicine and Surgery)
EBVS European Veterinary Specialist in Avian Medicine and Surgery
After graduating from the University of Queensland in 1995, Deborah Monks worked 
in both small animal and avian practice, attaining ANZCVS Membership in Avian Health 
(Cage & Aviary Bird) in 1999. In 2002, she moved to the UK and commenced an avian 
residency, culminating in becoming a Diplomate of the European College of Zoological 
Medicine (Avian) and a Fellow of the Australian and New Zealand College of Veterinary 
Scientists (Avian Medicine and Surgery) in 2006. She then opened her Brisbane Bird 
and Exotics Veterinary Service, seeing exclusively avian and exotic pets. Since starting 
her own practice, she has become very interested in guinea pig medicine and surgery, 
finding guinea pigs to be marvellous pets and complex patients. Her current interests 
include guinea pig dental disease and otitis media, as well as providing stress-reduced 
patient experiences.
Anna Meredith OBE MA VetMB PhD CertLAS DipZooMed DipECZM FRCVS
RCVS Specialist in Zoological Medicine
Anna Meredith is a Professor of Zoological and Conservation Medicine, and currently 
Executive Dean of the Faculty of Natural Sciences at Keele University. She graduated 
from Cambridge University in 1991 and worked at the Royal (Dick) School of Veterinary 
Studies for over three decades where she established the Exotic Animal and Wildlife 
Service. Between 2018 and 2022, Anna was Head of Melbourne University Veterinary 
School in Australia. She has extensive clinical, teaching and research experience 
with exotic pets, wildlife and conservation medicine, and has edited and co-authored 
several BSAVA Manuals.   
While the publishers, editors and contributors have used their best efforts in preparing this publication, the 
content is provided “as is”, for use by veterinary practitioners in the course of their practice and without 
warranty or responsibility. To the fullest extent permitted by law, BSAVA shall not be liable for any use made 
of this publication. In particular, the publishers, editors and contributors take no responsibility for information 
provided on dosages and methods of application of drugs mentioned or referred to in this publication.
